# terrakube_registry_module (Data Source)

Looks up a module published in the organization's Terrakube registry by name and provider.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "terrakube_organization" "org" {
  name = "my-org"
}

data "terrakube_registry_module" "label" {
  organization_id = data.terrakube_organization.org.id
  name            = "label"
  provider_name   = "null"
}

output "label_source" {
  value = data.terrakube_registry_module.label.source
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Module name
- `organization_id` (String) Terrakube organization ID
- `provider_name` (String) Module provider name (e.g. `aws`, `azurerm`, `null`). The triple `{organization}/{name}/{provider_name}` is what uniquely identifies a module in the registry.

### Read-Only

- `description` (String) Module description
- `folder` (String) Subfolder containing the module sources, if any
- `id` (String) Module ID
- `latest_version` (String) Latest version detected by Terrakube's VCS scan
- `registry_path` (String) Registry path in the form `{organization}/{name}/{provider}`
- `source` (String) Source Git URL of the module
- `ssh_id` (String) SSH key ID, if the module is private
- `tag_prefix` (String) Tag prefix for mono-repository modules
- `vcs_id` (String) VCS connection ID, if the module is private
